violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 08/04/2025
The hand-washing sink at the bar was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/04/2025

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink at the bar. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/04/2025

Raw animal foods (shelled eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (deli meats) in the reach-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/04/2025

(Various chemical's) was stored with food, equipment, utensils, or single service items. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/04/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the drying rack & 3 bay sink have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/04/2025

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2025
The ceiling in the kitchen area is in poor repair, inspector observed missing tiles. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2025

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen , is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

There was no evidence that employees are required to report to the person in charge information about their health (illnesses, symptoms) as they relate to diseases that are transmissible through food. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

Per interview of staff, ice cream scoops are only washed and rinsed at the ice cream window 3-bay sink. The surfaces of equipment that contacts time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be washed, rinsed, and sanitized. Inspector showed employees how to sanitize equipment at the 3-bay sink at the ice cream window. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Ice machine was taken out of service and must not be used until it is cleaned. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink in the kitchen was 100 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200 ppm. A new sanitizing solution was mixed to 200 ppm during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (Del's Lemonade machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Del's lemonade was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Dels Lemonade machine was taken out of service and must not be used until it is cleaned. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

Caesar dressing was observed held at 54 degrees Fahrenheit out of refrigeration at the prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, Caesar dressing had been out for 20 minutes. Caesar dressing was placed under refrigeration to cool during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

Utensils (ice cream scoops) were observed stored in stagnant water at 90 degrees Fahrenheit between uses at the ice cream window. Utensils must be hot held at 135 degrees Fahrenheit. Utensils were removed from water and water was heated to 136 during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

Hot water is not available at the handwashing sinks in the bathrooms located outside and the handwashing sink in at the ice cream window. Hot water must be available for handwashing. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024
The handwashing sink in the kitchen is in poor repair. Cold water is not turning on. Handwashing sink must be repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2023

There was no evidence that employees are required to report to the person in charge information about their health (illnesses, symptoms) as they relate to diseases that are transmissible through food. An employee illness policy was given to the person in charge during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2023

The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Written procedures were given to the person in charge during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2023
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2023
